subject: grammar
title: Replacing Phrases with Adverbs
instruction: Replace the underlined part of each sentence with an adverb from the box (actually, apparently, evidently, frankly, on the whole, possibly, surprisingly, unfortunately) that has a similar meaning.
passage: Example: I have heard that he is a teacher. -> Apparently

questions.text: <u>To be honest</u> I do not really like this kind of literature.
<u>I found it unexpected that</u> the survey revealed that university students today spend longer on their work than in the past.
<u>I am not happy about it but</u> I cannot come to your party because I am working.
<u>To sum it up</u>, it seems that people prefer to spend festivals with their families or friends rather than alone.
<u>From what I have read in the journal</u>, it seems that scientists believe the new drug will work.
<u>I am not absolutely certain about this but</u> if we reduce the amount we consume, we will become healthier.
<u>As a matter of fact</u> I think this is the best piece of work you've done so far.
